title: Latency, Throughput, Memory, Caching, Load, and Cost · Behavior Case: Rag Stage Latency Budget
summary: A team optimizes average response time with a warm cache, ignores index memory and model queueing, retrieves more candidates on every request, and reports token price without total service cost. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stage latency budget increment in RAG capacity and cost envelope. Learners pro…
objectives: Allocate deadlines across admission, query processing, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, streaming, and cleanup with p50, p95, and p99 evidence.
Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-performance-cost-capacity-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-performance-cost-capacity-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_performance_cost_capacity_5(queries_per_second: int = 24, retrieval_ms: int = 45, rerank_ms: int = 38, generation_ms: int = 420, deadline_ms: int = 650, cost_microunits: int = 950, cache_hit: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if queries_per_second <= 0 or min(retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, generation_ms) < 0:
        return False, 0, "capacity-input"
    total_ms = retrieval_ms + rerank_ms + generation_ms
    if total_ms > deadline_ms:
        return False, total_ms, "latency-budget"
    if cost_microunits < 0:
        return False, cost_microunits, "cost-accounting"
    adjusted_cost = cost_microunits // 2 if cache_hit else cost_microunits
    assert adjusted_cost <= cost_microunits
    return True, adjusted_cost, "capacity-envelope"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Stage Latency Budget: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 9342 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
